session_summary: Session built the Sympy proof-verification notebook then pivoted to scaffolding the canonical sourced derivation (derivation.qmd) in stages: notation, ledger, full tagged proof. Marcus drove almost everything: he reframed the deliverable toward a fully formalized axiom/lemma registry, demanded equation- and line-level provenance for every step, controlled scope (stay MVN), interrogated notation choices, and rejected house jargon and symbol collisions. The AI executed and repeatedly surfaced decisions; Marcus's interrogation caught unsourced steps and a phi double-duty notation bug.

coded_turns.quote:
build a Sympy + Quarto notebook that symbolically verifies the
  central theorem (Q-function deviance-bias decomposition), per the
  operational spec in todo/007
coded_turns.note: Detailed kickoff prompt: builds Sympy verification notebook, with honest-scoping and no-silent-fallback discipline imposed.

coded_turns.quote: How is this gonna be useful in the paper?
coded_turns.note: Probes the deliverable's purpose, forcing justification of the notebook's role in the manuscript.

coded_turns.quote: I want something where we list all axioms and properties that will be used, and I mean all like linearity properties of expectation or iterated expectation, regularity conditions, matrix algebra
coded_turns.note: Reframes deliverable: demands a fully formalized axiom/lemma registry referenced in the derivation.

coded_turns.quote: We should probably do any citation searching, including what we already have before drafting in full.
coded_turns.note: Sequences citation acquisition before full drafting; names existing sources (Matrix Cookbook, Shafer, van der Vaart).

coded_turns.quote: But you should do it strategically to be smart with context. We can search by embedding with litrev or you can search the Md with keywords
coded_turns.note: Approves search-first with a context-efficiency constraint and two suggested methods.

coded_turns.quote: Isn’t the missing information principle on Shafer. I know it isn’t the source
coded_turns.note: Supplies a likely source location for the missing-information principle, correcting the citation target.

coded_turns.quote: Are you sure that vech is not already in the matrix cookbook?
coded_turns.note: Stress-tests the claim that vech is absent from the cookbook, forcing a verification check.

coded_turns.quote: What’s the title for Magnus–Neudecker
coded_turns.note: Routine request for the reference title to add it to Zotero.

coded_turns.quote: Ok I’ll add this it’ll just be a bit when I get home
coded_turns.note: Logistics: commits to acquiring the Magnus-Neudecker PDF later.

coded_turns.quote: Alright. I have added the referennce and pdf into zotero
coded_turns.note: Reports the Magnus-Neudecker reference and PDF are now in Zotero for the pipeline.

coded_turns.quote: We need to migrate it from zotero to google drive using litrev and then copy over the md from mathpix / litsync skill
coded_turns.note: Specifies the canonical acquisition pipeline rather than reading the Zotero PDF directly.

coded_turns.quote: First let's start with defining notation and quantities, ensuring whever possible we trace that convention back to sources we have and/or making sure we are clear by what we mean by things like RIV. Second, we do the ledger. Then we move to the full derivation.
coded_turns.note: Sets the three-stage document order (notation, ledger, derivation) with source-traced conventions.

coded_turns.quote: NOw it isn't enough to just cite the reference. You need to provide equations from the reference that you are invoking. If an equation is not available, provide the line numbber from the markdown.
coded_turns.note: Tightens provenance standard: every invoked source needs its equation or markdown line number.

coded_turns.quote: since this is an html file can we do hoverovers for or box4es on provenance. Provenance is important, but it is really affecting readability
coded_turns.note: Flags provenance clutter hurting readability; proposes hover/box presentation to separate audit detail.

coded_turns.quote: I like the side margin! very nice.
coded_turns.note: Approves the side-margin provenance presentation option.

coded_turns.quote: Why are we introducing X in D1 instead of just using Y?
coded_turns.note: Questions an unnecessary notation symbol in definition D1, probing the notation design.

coded_turns.quote: How is it that you applied linearity of trace, and tr(I_k) = K but you didn't give provenance to Matrix Cookbook?
coded_turns.note: Catches an unsourced algebra step; enforces the no-unsourced-step rule he just imposed.

coded_turns.quote: Should we add a hook?
coded_turns.note: Raises whether to enforce provenance via an automated hook; AI counsels a plain script instead.

coded_turns.quote: Ok let’s go to stage 2
coded_turns.note: Defers the provenance hook and advances to Stage 2 (ledger).

coded_turns.quote: What is this: explicitly labeled as the lemma whose misuse generates the spurious −½k; MI variance recovery (Meng Lemma 3 + Main result)?
coded_turns.note: Queries a conflated ledger entry, surfacing that L2 and L4 are two distinct lemmas run together.

coded_turns.quote: Show this again
coded_turns.note: Asks AI to re-display the lemma content for inspection.

coded_turns.quote: Can you render as a pdf and show me here so I can open it on my phone
coded_turns.note: Requests a PDF render to review the document on mobile.

coded_turns.quote: Don’t worry about it
coded_turns.note: Drops the PDF render after AI flags a TeX Live version mismatch; accepts HTML fallback.

coded_turns.quote: Okay do the fully tagged proof
coded_turns.note: Greenlights Stage 3, the full tagged proof.

coded_turns.quote: Why in D5 are you invoking Q^bar when defining the complete data likelihood? Y not just \scriptl(\theta|Y)?
coded_turns.note: Catches a definitional mismatch in D5; fix tightens I_com definition and Stage 3 convergence claim.

coded_turns.quote: why are we limiting ourselves to something that specific and not go for the more general case, invoke the quadratic approximation, etc?
coded_turns.note: Questions the MVN-only restriction, asking whether to pursue the general quadratic-approximation case.

coded_turns.quote: Maybe we just keep in the normal case for now and if a reviewer pushes back to make more general, we do that then. Let's keep scope in control
coded_turns.note: Decides to keep MVN scope, deferring generalization until a reviewer demands it.

coded_turns.quote: Things like "W1 fixture" are nto going to make sense. 2. Some equations should have numbers associated with theme--things that are major and would show up in a proof sketch in the main section
coded_turns.note: Rejects internal codename jargon and requires numbered equations for proof-sketch-worthy results.

coded_turns.quote: Let's commit
coded_turns.note: Approves committing the D5/D6/D7 fix plus jargon and equation-numbering pass.

coded_turns.quote: Fix the hook to whitelist eq/fig/tbl/sec prefixes
coded_turns.note: Orders the citation hook fixed to allow Quarto crossref prefixes so equation cross-refs work.

coded_turns.quote: in stage 1 we put provenance in the margins (but not for stage 2 and 3). I want provenance on the margin
coded_turns.note: Requires consistent margin-provenance presentation across all three stages.

coded_turns.quote: I'm still seeing "W1" showing up
coded_turns.note: Audits the render and catches residual W1 codename still visible to readers.

coded_turns.quote: It looks like \phi is doing a double duty as both the normal pdf and a simulated parameter estimate. I think I rather have the normal pdf just be an f_{\scriptN}
coded_turns.note: Catches phi notation collision (normal pdf vs imputation draw); demands disambiguation.

coded_turns.quote: Why not something like \phi^\hat^\tilde or some thing?
coded_turns.note: Extends the notation challenge, probing why phi-tilde rather than a hat-tilde for the simulated estimate.

coded_turns.quote: we should put all verbatim text references from the sources into an appendix organized by reference, link to that section/subsection in the appendix
coded_turns.note: Specifies a verbatim-source appendix plus Petersen-Pedersen equation-number citations for the next session.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: next-session todo: verbatim-source appendix + Matrix Cookbook equation numbers

coded_turns.quote: give me the prompt for the next session
coded_turns.note: Requests a self-contained handoff prompt for the next session.

ai_side.evidence: Want me to draft that footnote and the Discussion paragraph when §3 gets written?
ai_side.note: Proposed a two-artifact verification framing and offered to draft the footnote.

ai_side.evidence: the overselling is the thing that gets AI-assisted papers torn apart in review
ai_side.note: Flagged the overselling risk for AI-assisted papers and recommended a defensible claim.

ai_side.evidence: should this ledger become *the* canonical derivation that supersedes MI-IC's v4.5
the only thing I still need from you is the A8 call (Option A + acquire Magnus–Neudecker, or Option B coordinate-free with Anderson)
a Schafer-literate referee will misread the quantity
I'm giving this title from training knowledge, so it stays `proposed` in `todo/003` until a PDF is acquired
no Mathpix cache for this PDF, and it's a 47-page excerpt, not the full book
Which do you want as the default — C (margin), B (collapsible), or a C-primary/B-overflow hybrid?
the existing `check_citations.py` already broke once on a working-directory edge case... More hooks = more of that fragility.
the margin notes need the `sidenotes` LaTeX package, and TinyTeX can't fetch it
is (2) something you want to take on, or leave flagged as the open frontier?
For §3 to cite "equation (6)," either reference by literal number, or... teach `check_citations.py` to whitelist Quarto's reserved crossref prefixes
I treated the inline `[ledger-tag]` references as proof structure (kept inline), not provenance (margin). If you'd rather those go to the margin too... say so
